title: Notebook-first medical ML is still real research
excerpt: The Alzheimer MRI project is not a polished package, and that is exactly why it is useful to show. The notebook preserves the real experimental workflow: preprocessing, augmentation, CNN training, and ensemble comparison in one visible record.

links.label: Alzheimer MRI project
links.description: Project page with notebook and research framing.

pageSections.title: A notebook can be the honest form of the work
pageSections.markdown:
There is a tendency to believe that only polished libraries count as serious technical artifacts. In research, that is often backwards. The notebook can be the more honest object because it preserves sequence: what the dataset looked like, how preprocessing was done, what assumptions were hardcoded, where evaluation happened, and how ensemble variants were introduced.

That is exactly why the Alzheimer MRI repository should be shown as a notebook-first case study rather than rewritten into a fake product package.

pageSections.title: What the portfolio should highlight
pageSections.markdown:
The most interesting thing to preserve on a portfolio is not a claim that the model works in the abstract. It is the visible method: augmentation choices, dataset split logic, and how the notebook evolves from a single classifier toward comparison and ensemble experiments.

That kind of visibility makes the project easier to evaluate because it shows how the work was actually done.
